gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (52/ 9)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (52/ 9)
text: مَنْ زَارَ أَخَاهُ لِلَّهِ لَا لِغَيْرِهِ الْتِمَاسَ مَوْعِدِ اللَّهِ وَ تَنَجُّزَ مَا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ وَكَّلَ اللَّهُ بِهِ سَبْعِينَ أَلْفَ مَلَكٍ يُنَادُونَهُ أَلَا طِبْتَ وَ طَابَتْ لَكَ الْجَنَّةُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ali Bin Fazzal, from Ali Bin Uqba, from Abu Hamza,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who visits his brother for the Sake of Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> not for anything else, seeking the Promise of Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> and accomplishing what is in the Presence of Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> would Allocate seventy thousand Angels calling out to him: ‘Indeed! You have done well, and the Paradise is (Made to be) better for you’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Ali ibn Faddal from Ali ibn ‘Aqaba from abu Hamza from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Whoever visits his brother (in belief) for the sake of Allah and for no other reason, seeking thereby the promise of Allah, and to achieve what is with Allah, Allah will appoint seventy thousand angels who applaud, ‘How beautiful is what you have done and how beautiful is paradise (for you).”’
